other_findings.finding: Migratory DC signature is conserved across species (murine dermal DC2 and splenic DC1)
Migratory DC signature is present in disease states including tonsillitis, ascites, lung cancer and rheumatoid arthritis
Acquisition of migratory DC gene signature is associated with loss of genes conferring DC subset identity
Previously reported in fetal thymic medullary DCs, suggesting developmental gene programs utilized in adult homeostasis

evidence_quotes.quote: The clusters at the convergence of these branches (moDC3, LC4 and migratory DC (Mig. DC)) express transcripts associated with DC maturation (CD83, CCR7, LAMP3, CD40, CD86), immunoregulation (CD274, IDO1, CD200, PDCD1LG2, SOCS1) and migration (FSCN1, PLGRKT, TRAF1, BCL2A1, CFLAR and REL)
the migratory DC signature is also present across disease states including tonsillitis, ascites, lung cancer and rheumatoid arthritis
